extraction.one_line_summary: CAVAGNA Remy attacked from the break at 25km out, survived cobbled climb into Toledo while GC group neutralized after early crash split.
tactical_patterns: Early crash neutralization favored GC leader—Roglic's team controlled pace after Bennett/Martin crash split peloton despite tactical opportunity.
Cavagna's 25km solo on technical cobbled finish worked because peloton prioritized neutralization over chasing—rare window created by crash.
CCC led peloton in final kilometers for Bennett sprint despite stage already lost—team commitment to sprinter even when tactically irrelevant.

rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: negative: crashed early creating Jumbo-Visma tactical deficit that should have pressured Roglic's defense
rider_signals.BENNETT George: negative: crashed in same early incident compromising Jumbo-Visma's domestique depth for final stages
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remy: positive: attacked 25km out and held technical cobbled climbing finish alone—diesel engine strength underestimated in breakaway scenarios
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: gained only 3 seconds on Roglic despite crash-induced split—passive when tactical chaos created rare opportunity
